1: Sector one is the run to turn two — brake straight, get it slowed, and prioritise the exit onto the climb.
Time in sector one comes from turn two and three — square them off, rotate early, and get on the power clean.
2: Sector two is the climb — keep the momentum through five and six, and don't scrub speed fighting the camber.
Through the middle sector, carry your speed up the hill — a tidy turn six sets up the whole top section.
3: Sector three is the Corkscrew and the plunge — brake in a straight line at the top, use the kerbs, and let it flow down to eleven.
Last sector: patience into the Corkscrew, then commit downhill — and get eleven right, it feeds the whole front straight.

1: Sector one — don't overwork La Source; the lap is the run through Eau Rouge, so exit wide and flat where the car allows.
Keep it simple in sector one: clean hairpin, full commitment up Raidillon, then hold your line down the Kemmel.
2: Sector two is rhythm — Les Combes through Rivage, then trust the grip in Pouhon. That double-left is where the lap lives.
The middle sector rewards patience at Rivage and bravery in Pouhon — one more click of commitment there is free time.
3: Sector three: carry everything you dare through Blanchimont, then nail the Bus Stop kerbs without unsettling the car.
Final sector, two jobs — flat through Blanchimont, tidy through the chicane. Don't give the lap away at the last corner.

1: Sector one is braking and traction — hit your marker for the first chicane, use the kerbs, and get the run through Curva Grande.
Big stop into turn one — brake deep but straight, and make the exit the priority, the whole sector is the straight after.
2: Sector two: rhythm through the Lesmos — sacrifice entry on the second one for exit speed onto the back section.
Middle sector is the Roggia chicane and the Lesmos — attack the kerbs at Roggia, but keep the second Lesmo tidy.
3: Sector three is Ascari and Parabolica — smooth through Ascari, then open the wheel early in Parabolica and use every inch of exit.
The lap is won at Parabolica — in a touch slower, out a lot faster, all the way to the line.

1: Sector one flows from Abbey through the Loop — commit through the fast opening, then be disciplined in the slow stuff.
Keep it flat where you can early in the lap, then get the Loop rotated properly — the exit feeds the Wellington straight.
2: Sector two is Maggotts and Becketts — build the rhythm, sacrifice the first apex to be quick through the last one onto Hangar.
The middle sector lives and dies in Becketts: smooth hands, one line, and carry everything onto the Hangar straight.
3: Sector three: brake straight into Stowe, and treat Club as two corners — patient in, early power out to the line.
Last sector is Stowe and Vale — don't overdrive Stowe, and get the final complex straightened early for the run home.

1: Sector one is Tarzan and Hugenholtz — use the banking, let the car roll speed, and don't fight the front end.
Into Tarzan, brake late but keep it on line — the banking will hold you. Exit speed there is the whole first sector.
2: Sector two through Scheivlak is commitment — trust the grip over the crest and keep your minimum speed up through the dunes.
The middle sector is fast and blind — memorise your references, because lifting where it's flat costs you all the way.
3: Sector three: use the banked final corner properly — high line, early throttle, and let it sling you down the straight.
Final sector is about the last banking — get on the power sooner than feels natural, the banking carries it.

1: Sector one is Paddock Hill — brave on entry, let it run down the hill, then get Druids slowed properly. Two very different corners.
Paddock Hill Bend is the money corner early on — commit over the crest and use the compression at the bottom.
2: Sector two flows through the back section — keep the momentum, and be tidy where the track drops away off camber.
In the middle sector, smooth is fast — the surface falls away on exit, so feed the power in progressively.
3: Sector three is Sheene and Clearways — sacrifice entry at Clearways for the drive onto the pit straight, that run is everything.
The lap finishes on the Clearways exit — get the car straight early and carry the run all the way to the line.

1: Sector one: quick right at turn one, then it's all about the run down the Parabolika — clean exit, then breathe.
Don't overdrive turn one — a tidy exit there is a long, long straight of free time.
2: Sector two is the hairpin — brake deep and straight, rotate it, and prioritise traction out onto the return straight.
Middle sector: the hairpin decides it. In slow, pivot, out hard — and use all the exit kerb.
3: Sector three is the stadium — keep the rhythm through Mobil 1 and the Sachs Kurve, and be patient onto the power in the final right.
The stadium section rewards tidiness — short corners, quick direction changes, and a disciplined final exit.

1: Sector one: attack the Tamburello kerbs but keep the car settled — the run to Villeneuve and Tosa is where the sector's decided.
Take what the Tamburello chicane gives, then make Tosa the priority — slow in, rotated, and hard up the hill.
2: Sector two is Piratella and Acque Minerali — carry speed through the top, then use the downhill compression into Acque.
The middle sector flows downhill — Piratella entry sets up everything, don't pinch it.
3: Sector three: be aggressive over the Variante Alta kerbs, then double-apex Rivazza with the exit as the only priority.
The lap ends on the Rivazza exit — give up entry speed and get the power down early for the run to the line.

1: Sector one is the Esses — one rhythm, one line, minimum steering. Force it once and you lose the whole sequence.
Through the first sector let the car flow — the Esses reward patience and punish the first greedy apex.
2: Sector two: tidy through the Degners — they bite. Then straighten the hairpin and prioritise the drive to Spoon.
Middle sector is Degner discipline and a clean hairpin — both punish overdriving more than they reward bravery.
3: Sector three: commit through 130R, then make the chicane about exit — the run to the line is long.
Spoon exit and 130R set up the final sector — carry everything you can, then be surgical in the last chicane.

1: Sector one is short and brutal — brake straight up the hill into turn one and get the power down early over the crest.
Uphill braking into turn one — the car will take more than you think. Exit clean, the straight is long.
2: Sector two is the Remus hairpin — deep, straight braking, rotate it hard, and don't spin it up on the exit over the crest.
The middle sector is decided at Remus — in slow and rotated beats in fast and wide every single lap.
3: Sector three flows through the fast rights — keep the minimum speed up and use the exit kerbs on the final two corners.
The last two rights are the lap — commit to the penultimate one and let the final corner sling you to the line.

1: Sector one: brake late into turn one but make the exit count, then be brave through the downhill left at turn four.
Turn one is an overtaking spot, but on a clean lap the priority is the exit and the run down to the valley.
2: Sector two never stops turning — link the corners, keep the car balanced, and don't let one scruffy apex ruin four.
The middle sector is rhythm — it's a go-kart section, so keep your hands calm and the speed rolling.
3: Sector three: get the penultimate corner right — it's the setup for the final turn and the whole pit straight.
The last corner is long — patient entry, early throttle, and use every centimetre of exit kerb to the line.

1: Sector one is the climb — clean out of Hell Corner, use all of Mountain Straight, then be precise up through the Cutting.
Up the mountain, precision beats bravery — the walls don't move and the sector's won in the Cutting.
2: Sector two is across the top — rhythm through the Esses and Skyline, no sudden inputs, the car's never straight up there.
Across the top of the mountain: commit where you know, survive where you don't. Time up there is earned in trust.
3: Sector three: tidy through Forrest's Elbow — every mistake there is paid down all of Conrod — then nail the Chase.
The Elbow feeds Conrod Straight — exit speed there is worth more than anywhere else on this mountain.

1: Sector one is the Senna S — brake deep downhill, keep it tidy through the left, and carry the run down to Descida do Lago.
The Senna S decides your first sector — sacrifice the first apex a touch to get the drive out of the second.
2: Sector two is the infield — keep the momentum through the middle, and mind the compression at the bottom of the hill.
The middle sector twists back on itself — smooth steering and early vision, always looking two corners ahead.
3: Sector three is the climb to the line — get Juncao right and the uphill drag does the rest, it's the longest full-throttle run.
Everything in the final sector is Juncao — slow hands in, early power out, and hold it flat all the way up the hill.

1: Sector one: carry speed through the fast right at turn three — it's the corner that defines the opening sector.
Brake clean into turn one, but the sector is really turn three — commit to it and the time follows.
2: Sector two is the technical middle — get turn five rotated, then keep the rhythm through the uphill sweeps.
The middle sector rewards a settled car — smooth through the long corners, and don't ask for everything at once.
3: Sector three is the final chicane and the last corner — traction is everything, get it straight and drive to the line.
The lap finishes on traction — be disciplined in the chicane and prioritise the run onto the pit straight.
